description: Consisting of many interconnected parts; intricate rather than simple. 'Ruka' names the quality of rich interconnection—systems with many relationships, patterns with multiple layers, wholes made of interlocking parts.
sound_symbolism: The word weaves connections. 'Ru' begins with rolling intricacy, parts gathering. Then 'ka' clicks into place, elements interlocking.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'ruka nuri' (complex cycle). Opposite of 'siloma' (simple). Not negative—complexity can be beautiful, as in ecosystems or Art Nouveau designs.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Complex systems like ecosystems demonstrate resilience through interconnection—complexity as strength.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Art Nouveau embraced complex organic patterns—intricate designs that revealed new details on each viewing.
